title: Battery Thermal Drift Response
content: When battery_temp_c exceeds threshold for more than two consecutive windows, reduce duty cycle by 30 percent, inspect cooling ducts for debris, verify fan response, and schedule maintenance within 24 hours if temperature does not normalize after one reduced-load cycle.

title: Motor Current Spike Triage
content: For sustained motor_current_a spikes, inspect payload weight, wheel drag, gearbox resistance, and actuator alignment. If current remains elevated after unloading, remove the robot from service for mechanical inspection.

title: Network Latency and Roaming Recovery
content: When network_latency_ms spikes above the service threshold, verify access point congestion, roaming between SSIDs, packet retransmits, and upstream switch errors. Reduce non-critical telemetry bursts and pin the robot to the nearest healthy zone if packet loss accompanies the latency event.

title: CPU Thermal Throttle Response
content: When cpu_temp_c approaches thermal limits, confirm fan duty cycle, heatsink seating, dust loading, and background process pressure. Pause non-critical jobs, keep safety monitors online, and restore full compute load only after the temperature trend is stable for two reporting windows.

title: Disk IO Wait Investigation
content: When disk_io_wait_ms exceeds normal operating bands, inspect container log growth, filesystem saturation, and storage latency alarms. Clear runaway logs, rotate large traces, and schedule storage replacement if wait time stays elevated during low load.

title: Sensor Drift Calibration Procedure
content: When sensor_drift_pct increases gradually across multiple windows, compare the live reading against a calibrated reference, check harness seating, and perform an in-place calibration cycle before returning the unit to autonomous duty.

title: Wheel Slip Recovery
content: If wheel_slip_ratio exceeds the traction threshold, inspect floor contamination, tire wear, and load balance. Reduce acceleration, reroute around the affected lane, and dispatch a floor check if slip persists on multiple robots in the same zone.

title: Safe Emergency Shutdown Verification
content: When a critical anomaly suggests imminent hardware damage, stop the affected robot in place, preserve event logs, confirm the safety interlock status, and require manual clearance before power restoration.

title: Fleet Reroute and Zone Isolation
content: When multiple robots in the same zone report related anomalies, isolate the zone from new assignments, reroute active units to a healthy corridor, and notify operators to inspect shared infrastructure such as access points, conveyors, or charging rails.

title: Scheduled Maintenance Escalation
content: If a robot exceeds any maintenance threshold for the third time in seven days, schedule the next available maintenance slot, attach the incident history, and keep the robot on reduced duty until the work order is completed.

title: Robot 03 Battery Thermal Drift
content: Robot-03 reported repeated battery_temp_c excursions during afternoon picking cycles. Retrieval evidence showed the fan command responded correctly but cooling airflow stayed flat. Maintenance found packaging debris packed into the lower cooling duct. After cleaning the duct and reducing duty cycle for one shift, the thermal trend returned to baseline.

title: Robot 11 Battery Bay Heat Soak
content: Robot-11 experienced high battery temperature during rapid dock-turnaround cycles near a loading bay door with elevated ambient heat. The incident was resolved by redistributing charge windows and replacing a partially obstructed intake filter on the battery enclosure.

title: Robot 07 Motor Current Spike
content: Robot-07 showed recurring motor_current_a spikes while moving loaded totes between two conveyor zones. Operators initially suspected the payload, but maintenance found increasing actuator resistance caused by a misaligned belt guide. Current normalized after guide adjustment and a short reduced-load observation period.

title: Robot 02 Drive Load Drift
content: Robot-02 reported medium motor current drift over several shifts. Historical data showed the spikes followed long uphill ramp usage. The root cause was wheel drag from debris caught around the rear caster; cleaning and rebalancing the route profile resolved the issue.

title: Robot 12 Vibration Escalation
content: Robot-12 produced rising vibration_rms over four hours. Inspection confirmed a loose motor mount and early bearing wear on the drive side. Torque correction stabilized the vibration trend, and the bearing was replaced during the next maintenance window.

title: Zone C Network Latency Wave
content: Multiple robots in Zone C recorded high network_latency_ms at the same time, with packet retransmits and roaming events. Root cause analysis found a degraded access point uplink and aggressive telemetry bursts during shift change. Replacing the uplink and rate-limiting non-critical traffic cleared the issue.

title: Robot 15 CPU Thermal Saturation
content: Robot-15 hit repeated cpu_temp_c alarms while running vision inference and local replay diagnostics together. Logs showed a background trace exporter and image preprocessing job contending for CPU. Disabling the non-critical job and reseating the heatsink eliminated throttling.

title: Robot 06 Sensor Drift After Impact
content: Robot-06 displayed slowly increasing sensor_drift_pct after a low-speed shelving impact. A reference check showed the lidar mast was slightly out of alignment. Recalibration corrected the readings and no additional drift was observed after the mount was tightened.

title: Robot 09 Wheel Slip on Spill Zone
content: Robot-09 experienced repeated wheel_slip_ratio alerts on the same corridor immediately after a packaging fluid spill. The fleet was rerouted, the floor was cleaned, and traction returned to normal without hardware replacement.

title: Robot 14 Disk IO Wait Escalation
content: Robot-14 recorded elevated disk_io_wait_ms and intermittent telemetry lag during overnight diagnostics. The storage volume was nearly saturated by verbose logs and retained trace files. Rotating logs and clearing stale artifacts restored IO latency to normal levels.
